description_long: This is a head of Savoy cabbage, characterized by its large, deeply crinkled, and overlapping green leaves that form a rounded, somewhat irregular ball. The outer leaves are dark green with prominent white veins, while the inner leaves appear lighter and more tightly packed. The surface texture is highly textured and bumpy, with a matte finish and visible undulations along the leaf margins. The cabbage appears fresh, with no visible blemishes or discoloration, and the leaves are robust and slightly curled at the edges. The overall structure is dense, with the leaves layered in a rosette pattern, creating a compact and sturdy vegetable. This cabbage would be commonly found in urban markets, grocery stores, or as part of urban gardening plots.
description: A round, densely layered Savoy cabbage with dark green, crinkled leaves and prominent white veins. The surface is highly textured and the head is compact and fresh.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, veined outer leaves with a deep green color and pronounced crinkles.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the top of the cabbage, revealing the tightly packed inner leaves and the layered structure.
description_view_2: A side view displaying the overlapping, curled leaves and the rounded shape of the cabbage.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the dense, veined surface and the compactness of the head, with the leaf texture clearly visible.
